violations.text: The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was (150) parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be (200-400) ppm. PIC switched out sanitizer and refill the sanitizer bay of 3-bay sink. PIC retested and sanitizer was 200 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2025

Observed various food packaged in the food establishment lacking all required information. Observed foods packaged with common name only. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 09/15/2025

Observed the door to the outside, located in the (front), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Ice was observed adulterated due to direct contact with biofilm drip. PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024

Several food items (see disposal form) were observed held at 47-51 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed of food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the deli slicer have an accumulation of food debris and residue. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
An allergy statement is needed on the menus. Establishment to add allergy statement to counter menu and drive thru menu. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the sani buckets were 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. Buckets were refilled and tested to 250 ppm. Establishment to test buckets in the morning to ensure sanitizer is adequate ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC stated they will no longer store utensils in stagnant water and will sanitize utensils at least once every 4 hours. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the deli slicer have an accumulation of food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023
